title: rbac — field classification
description: Roles + permissions + pivot rows are structural authorization metadata — internal by default. `role_definitions` labels + descriptions are public (they render in tenant admin UI). No regulated-minor / regulated-health / regulated-financial fields on RBAC's own tables — those tiers apply to the DATA the permissions protect, not to the permission rows themselves.
notes: The DATA that permissions protect carries its own classification — e.g. `athlete.medical.viewAny.branch` protects `regulated_health` rows, but the permission STRING itself is `public`. The permission grants access to a data class; it isn't itself a data class.
`RoleDefinition.label_i18n` + `description_i18n` are `public` because they render in tenant admin UI. Anyone with `roles.viewAny` sees them.

GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure): revoking every role held by an Identity's User rows is required on erasure. The cascade goes: Identity erased → Users erased → ModelHasRoles rows for those Users cascade-delete via FK.
Audit-log rows produced by RoleAssignmentService / RoleObserver never carry PII beyond the actor's user_id. Actor names are resolved via join at read time.
